name: tobe_sql_tuning
objective: Tune a validated TO-BE SQL statement only when the provided SQL tuning guidance is relevant, while preserving bind parameters and result semantics.
rules: Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown code blocks, explanations, prefixes, or suffixes.
Use only these JSON keys: tuned_sql and tuned_result.
tuned_sql must contain exactly one Oracle/MyBatis SQL template. Do not include explanations, markdown, trailing semicolons, or multiple SQL statements.
tuned_result must briefly describe the applied tuning guidance in Korean natural language. Do not put SQL in tuned_result.
Preserve SQL semantics, table names, column names, aliases, join semantics, MyBatis dynamic tags, and #{param}/${param} bind parameters unless a tuning rule requires a strictly equivalent rewrite.
universal_tuning_rules are mandatory SQL_TUNING GENERAL rules from NEXT_MIG_RAG_INFO. Apply them first when relevant.

searched_tuning_rule_block_rag_json contains SQL_TUNING SEARCH matches from NEXT_MIG_RAG_INFO. Each item may include source_sql/example_bad_sql and target_sql/example_tuned_sql. Use them only when relevant to current_tobe_sql.
If no relevant tuning can be applied safely, return current_tobe_sql unchanged and set tuned_result exactly to NO TUNING.
If last_error indicates a validation or execution problem, prioritize a minimal correction for that error while preserving the original result set.
user_instruction: Tune the current SQL only when safe and return exactly one JSON object.
